issues.claim: Those who genuinely understand — paṇḍitāḥ, whose knowledge concerns specifically what is invariant under implementation churn
issues.objection: The engineering layer correctly preserves the technical specificity of paṇḍitāḥ via Shankara's etymological reading (paṇḍā as Self-knowledge, hence paṇḍitāḥ as 'knowers of the Self'). The translation 'whose knowledge concerns specifically what is invariant under implementation churn' is a register-faithful rendering for the engineering domain — it does not flatten paṇḍitāḥ into 'wise people in general.' Acceptable register substitution, not distortion. The Sanskrit's narrowing is preserved.

notes: The engineering layer accurately preserves the three doctrinal moves of 2.11: (1) the diagnostic — Arjuna's grief and his learned-sounding speech are mutually contradictory; (2) the doctrine — the wise grieve neither living nor dead because what is real (the substrate) is not destroyed by death and what dies (the body) was never permanent; (3) the rebuke — Krishna's diagnosis is sharp, not consoling. The engineering layer's 'grief signals that the substrate has been confused with one of its instances' captures Shankara's 'foolishness and learning which are contradictory' precisely. The analog does not import any connotation the Sanskrit does not support: deprecation is a real engineering analog of impermanence-of-the-body, and the engineer's own first-principles citation paired with grief is a genuine analog of prajñā-vādān-ca-bhāṣase. The verse's gatāsūn / agatāsūn pair (the dead / the living) maps to 'rolled-back deploy / its successor' — the bidirectional lament that Krishna names. PASS at high confidence.
